name: Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — circular from Monasterio de la Virgen del Monlora
name_original: Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — circular desde el Monasterio de la Virgen del Monlora
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Luna traverse a landscape characterized by rolling terrain, river valleys, and historical sites. The region features a mix of agricultural lands, scattered woodlands, and the occasional rocky outcrop, providing varied scenery for cyclists. Water bodies like reservoirs are also present, offering scenic routes along their shores. Best touring cycling routes around Luna The most popular touring cycling route is Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — circular from Monasterio de la Virgen del Monlora, a 19.7 miles (31.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical castles and a monastery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Aguarales de Valdemilaz - Piedratajada - Marracos — from Valpalmas, a moderate 17.2 miles (27.7 km) path. This route offers views of the local countryside and small villages. Local touring cyclists also love the Sotonera Reservoir – La Sotonera Reservoir loop from Puendeluna, a 29.7 miles (47.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Luna is defined by rolling hills, reservoir paths, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Luna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 90 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Luna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Luna?
A: Luna offers a diverse selection of touring cycling routes, with over 17 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's a suitable path for most cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Luna?
A: Touring cycling routes around Luna feature a varied landscape, characterized by rolling terrain, scenic river valleys, and agricultural lands. You'll also encounter scattered woodlands, occasional rocky outcrops, and routes along the shores of reservoirs, providing a mix of natural beauty and historical backdrops.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners in Luna?
A: While Luna's routes are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, there are no routes specifically listed as 'easy'. However, many of the moderate routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994065696/castillos-de-obano-y-yequera-circular-from-monasterio-de-la-virgen-del-monlora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — circular from Monasterio de la Virgen del Monlora&lt;/a&gt;, offer manageable distances and elevation gains that could be suitable for cyclists with some experience looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Luna?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Luna offers several difficult routes. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel Castle – Ardisa Dam loop from Alda de Monlora&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 100 km and involves significant elevation changes. Another demanding route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murillo de Gállego – View of Mallos de Riglos loop from Murillo de Gállego&lt;/a&gt;, known for its scenic views and considerable climbs.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Luna?
A: Yes, Luna features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994065696/castillos-de-obano-y-yequera-circular-from-monasterio-de-la-virgen-del-monlora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — circular from Monasterio de la Virgen del Monlora&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to start and end at the same point while exploring historical sites. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38183653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotonera Reservoir – La Sotonera Reservoir loop from Puendeluna&lt;/a&gt;, offering a scenic ride around the reservoir.

Q: What are some notable sights or attractions I can see while cycling in Luna?
A: Luna's touring cycling routes often pass by fascinating natural and historical attractions. You can encounter unique geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María de la Corona&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ardisa Dam&lt;/a&gt;, which are often integrated into the routes.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Luna?
A: The touring cycling routes in Luna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the varied terrain, the scenic reservoir paths, and the opportunity to explore historical landmarks along the way.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes in Luna typically take?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Luna varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994065696/castillos-de-obano-y-yequera-circular-from-monasterio-de-la-virgen-del-monlora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Obano y Yéquera&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours and 28 minutes, while longer, more difficult routes can extend to over 6 hours, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel Castle – Ardisa Dam loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that feature reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, Luna's landscape includes several water bodies, and some touring cycling routes are designed to showcase them. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38183653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotonera Reservoir – La Sotonera Reservoir loop from Puendeluna&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a beautiful ride around the reservoir with varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Luna?
A: Given the region's characteristics of high desert landscapes and generally mild-to-hot temperatures with cooling breezes, touring cycling in Luna is often enjoyable year-round. However, spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying abundant sunshine.

Q: Where can I find historical sites or castles along the routes?
A: Luna is rich in historical sites. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994065696/castillos-de-obano-y-yequera-circular-from-monasterio-de-la-virgen-del-monlora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — circular from Monasterio de la Virgen del Monlora&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores historical castles and a monastery. Additionally, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel Castle – Ardisa Dam loop from Alda de Monlora&lt;/a&gt; features the historic Biel Castle, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there routes that offer views of unique natural formations?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts distinctive natural formations. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murillo de Gállego – View of Mallos de Riglos loop from Murillo de Gállego&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning views of the dramatic Mallos de Riglos. You can also find the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt; along routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993999641/aguarales-de-valdemilaz-piedratajada-marracos-from-valpalmas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz - Piedratajada - Marracos — from Valpalmas&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
We are in the Monlora Sanctuary with impressive views of the Cinco Villas region and on clear days we will also see Moncayo.
There is a small parking lot and a restaurant (check dining hours) monlora.com

Nice church located in the town of Luna. It is Romanesque in style and we will find it near the Torre Palacio del Reloj de la Corona from the 11th century, built as a watchtower for the lands reconquered from the Moors.

Obano Castle is an Aragonese fortification built in the second half of the 11th century, during the reign of Sancho Ramírez. It is located in the municipality of Luna, in the Cinco Villas region. This Romanesque-style castle was built to guard the communication routes between Huesca and Ejea de los Caballeros.

The castle is located near the Arba de Biel River and is known for its defensive tower, which rises to approximately 16 meters in height. Throughout its history, the castle has belonged to the Knights Templar and the Monastery of San Juan de la Peña.

Yéquera Castle, also known as Yecra Castle or Lacorvilla Castle, is located in the uninhabited medieval village of Yéquera. Its origins date back to a 10th-century Muslim fortification. In 1093, the Aragonese king Sancho Ramírez ordered the construction of a fortification to secure control of the newly conquered lands.

Known village for being near the spectacular Aguarales of Valpalmas and being the cradle of Santiago Ramón y Cajal where the birth house and museum of the Spanish doctor and scientist and Nobel Prize in Medicine is still preserved

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Monlora and Viewpoint of the Cinco Villas
